audit_items.proof_trace: BlobSchedule deserialization and construction lack validation that max_blobs_per_block <= MAX_BLOB_COMMITMENTS_PER_BLOCK (4096). Config entries can specify arbitrary values (e.g., 5000) which are accepted without bounds checking, creating type-safety violations and potential consensus splits.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker provides ChainSpec config with BLOB_SCHEDULE entry containing max_blobs_per_block=5000. Nodes load config successfully, then accept blocks with 4500 blob commitments (passing runtime check at block_verification.rs:885) but violating SSZ VariableList constraints, causing divergent validation behavior.
